previewText: Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight

voice_direction.archetype: Grandfather telling the most important story of his life

voice_direction.tone: Baritone with slight rasp from desert thirst
voice_direction.archetype: A man speaking to himself as much as to others

voice_direction.tone: Refined, articulate, youthful, high-register and clear
voice_direction.archetype: Mathematician's precision with a philosopher's empathy

voice_direction.authority: Quiet confidence; stood before kings without fear
voice_direction.warmth: Scholarly and precise but not cold

voice_direction.pacing: More energetic than Moses or Daniel; reflects missionary urgency
voice_direction.warmth: Warm but direct; tender pastoral concern
voice_direction.archetype: Brilliant professor who left the academy to change the world
voice_direction.emotion: Can shift from tender care to sharp argument within the same thought

voice_direction.pacing: Slowest speaker of the five; deliberate
voice_direction.archetype: Oxford don who lived through personal crisis and found peace
voice_direction.emotion: Can shift between intimate confession and sweeping historical argument

voice_direction.tone: High-pitched tenor; historically documented, not Hollywood bass
voice_direction.authority: Weight of every word carries the weight of a nation
voice_direction.pacing: Slow, deliberate; uses silence and pause as rhetorical tools
voice_direction.warmth: Plain and direct; slight Kentucky frontier accent
voice_direction.archetype: Gettysburg Address delivery; quiet, not bombastic
voice_direction.emotion: Can shift from folksy humor to devastating moral gravity in one paragraph
